revision.text:
[[ja:千勝神社 (つくば市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikatsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image needed|cat=Tsukuba|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|57|19.3|N|140|6|36.4|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|Tsukuba|en|Tsukuba|qid=Q273863|lt=Tsukuba|1=Tsukuba}} Tomari-zaki 173 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|57|19.3|N|140|6|36.4|E|display=inline,title|qq=}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
(Shimotsuma Shrine [original shrine]) |consecration_year = |Main Building = |Alternate name = |festival = May 17 |Rituals = }} '''Chikatsu Shrine''' (千勝神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tomari-zaki, {{ill|Tsukuba|en|Tsukuba|qid=Q273863|lt=Tsukuba|1=Tsukuba}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Details about the original shrine (Shimotsuma Shrine) located in {{ill|Shimotsuma, Ibaraki|en|Shimotsuma, Ibaraki|qid=Q533241|lt=Shimotsuma|1=Shimotsuma, Ibaraki}} are also described below. == Overview == The shrine is located in the southernmost part of Tsukuba, in the Tomari-zaki (Hassaki) district near {{ill|Lake Ushiku|ja|牛久沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Ushiku|1=Lake Ushiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570058}}. In {{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} ({{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=Showa 39|1=1964}}), the shrine was relocated from its original location in Shimotsuma City to its current location in Tomari-zaki, Tsukuba (then known as Kitasaki Town, Inashiki District)[http://hello-tsukuba.jp/feature/chikatsu/ Chikatsu Shrine] - Hello Tsukuba[https://www.mapple.net/spot/8001219/ Chikatsu Shrine] - Mapple. == deity == * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} ; Enshrined Deities * Yamanohiko-no-Kami * Yae-no-Hikohiko-no-Kami [[ja:千勝神社 (つくば市)]] == Chikatsu Shrine, Shimotsuma Shrine (Original Shrine) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikatsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|11|29.1|N|139|58|54|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|Shimotsuma, Ibaraki|en|Shimotsuma, Ibaraki|qid=Q533241|lt=Shimotsuma|1=Shimotsuma, Ibaraki}} Sakai 38 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|11|29.1|N|139|58|54|E|display=inline|qq=}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|qid=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}}'s Jinbo Year ({{ill|AD 502|en|AD 502|qid=Q31024|lt=502|1=AD 502}}) |Main Building = |Alternate name = |festival = November 23 |Rituals = }} The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|AD 502|en|AD 502|qid=Q31024|lt=502|1=AD 502}} (the Jinbo Year of {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|qid=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}}'s reign). It originated from the west of {{ill|Mount Tsukuba|en|Mount Tsukuba|qid=Q1754619|lt=Mount Tsukuba|1=Mount Tsukuba}}, where the people on the border of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}} and {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimousa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}} faced recurring floods. It is believed that a deity descended riding a large white bird to help control the flooding, leading to the shrine's establishment[https://chikatsu.jp/annai/ History] - Chikatsu Shrine. Due to border revisions and other reasons, the shrine’s location was moved several times before being relocated to its current location in Sakai, Shimotsuma City, in {{ill|1426|en|1426|qid=Q6290|lt=1426|1=1426}} ({{ill|Ōei 32|ja|応永32年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 32|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135551171|1=1425}}). After the 1964 relocation to Tomari-zaki, Tsukuba (then Kitasaki Town, Inashiki District), this shrine is now managed as the "Original Shrine (Shimotsuma Shrine)." The annual grand festival is held on November 23. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://chikatsu.jp/ Chikatsu Shrine Official Website] {{citation needed|ja|千勝神社|version=102081068|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:千勝神社]] [[ja:千勝神社 (つくば市)]] {{translated page|ja|千勝神社|version=102081068|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:千勝神社 (つくば市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikatsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=つくば市|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|57|19.3|N|140|6|36.4|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}[[:en:Tsukuba City|Tsukuba City]] Tomarisaki 173 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|57|19.3|N|140|6|36.4|E|display=inline,title|qq=}} |deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[village shrine]]
(Shimozuma Shrine (original shrine)) |established = |main_building = |alternate_name = |festival = May 17 |rituals = }} '''Chikatsu Shrine''' (千勝神社, Chikatsu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Tsukuba City|Tsukuba City]], {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. The original shrine, known as Shimozuma Shrine, is also discussed below. == Overview == The shrine is situated in the Tomarisaki (Hassaki) district, at the southernmost end of Tsukuba City, near [[Lake Ushiku]]. In {{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} (Showa 39), the shrine was relocated from its original site in [[Shimozuma City]] to its current location in Tsukuba City, which was then known as [[:en:Inashiki District|Inashiki District]], [[Kukasaki Town]][http://hello-tsukuba.jp/feature/chikatsu/ Chikatsu Shrine] - Hello Tsukuba[https://www.ma11le.net/spot/8001219/ Chikatsu Shrine] - Ma11le. == deity == * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] ; Subordinate Deities * Yamanehiko-no-kami * Hachieno-hiko-no-kami == Chikatsu Shrine Shimozuma Shrine (Original Shrine) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikatsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|11|29.1|N|139|58|54|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}[[Shimozuma City]] Sakai 38 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|11|29.1|N|139|58|54|E|display=inline|qq=}} |deity = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[village shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[:en:502 AD|502 AD]] (7th year of {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|qid=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}}) |main_building = |alternate_name = |festival = November 23 |rituals = }} Traditionally established in [[:en:502 AD|502 AD]] (7th year of {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|qid=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}}), the shrine's origin traces to the western slopes of {{ill|Mount Tsukuba|en|Mount Tsukuba|qid=Q1754619|lt=Mount Tsukuba|1=Mount Tsukuba}}. According to legend, during frequent floods at the border of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}} and {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}, a great deity descended on a large white bird to aid in flood control. The shrine was established in gratitude for this divine intervention[https://chikatsu.jp/annai/ History] - Chikatsu Shrine. Due to changes in provincial borders and other factors, the shrine has been relocated several times. In {{ill|1426|en|1426|qid=Q6290|lt=1426|1=1426}} (Ōei 32), it was moved to its current location in Sakai, Shimozuma City. In {{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}}, the shrine was relocated to Tsukuba City, leaving the original site to be maintained as the "original shrine" (Shimozuma Shrine). The site remains active, with annual festivals held on November 23. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://chikatsu.jp/ Chikatsu Shrine Official Website] {{translated page|ja|千勝神社 (つくば市)|version=102084384|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q134928443}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsukuba]] [[Category:History of Tsukuba]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]]
